options_appraisal.description: Refuse concurrent permits on linked corridors. Stagger Levelling Up, utility, and maintenance works sequentially not simultaneously.
options_appraisal.legal_basis: NRSWA 1991 s59 (duty to coordinate); TMA 2004 s16 (Network Management Duty); Co-ordination Code 6th Ed 2025
options_appraisal.evidence: Feb-Mar 2026 Manchester Rd + Colne Rd + Hammerton St simultaneous closure = 'utterly unmanageable'

options_appraisal.description: Mandate utility works on traffic-sensitive roads between 8pm-6am using permit conditions. S56 NRSWA timing directions.
options_appraisal.legal_basis: NRSWA 1991 s56; Lancashire Permit Scheme conditions
options_appraisal.evidence: TfL night works policy reduced daytime disruption by 30%+ on A-roads

options_appraisal.description: Charge utilities up to £2,500/day for occupying traffic-sensitive roads during peak hours. Financial incentive for off-peak/night working.
options_appraisal.legal_basis: Traffic Management Act 2004 s32-39; Lane Rental Guidance 2025
options_appraisal.evidence: Kent/Surrey schemes reduced peak-hour utility works by 60-70%

options_appraisal.description: Charge utilities up to £10,000/day for overrunning works, now including weekends and bank holidays (loophole closed Jan 2026).
options_appraisal.legal_basis: NRSWA 1991 s74; 2025 Regulations extending to all days
options_appraisal.status: Weekend charging newly available from 5 Jan 2026

options_appraisal.description: Upgrade 2 critical junctions to adaptive signal control (SCOOT urban networks or MOVA isolated junctions). Real-time green time optimisation.
options_appraisal.status: Not deployed at most Burnley junctions
options_appraisal.evidence: MOVA typically increases junction capacity by 10-15%. SCOOT reduces delays by 12% average

options_appraisal.description: After resurfacing a road, prohibit further excavation for up to 12 months. Prevents utilities immediately digging up new surfaces.
options_appraisal.legal_basis: NRSWA 1991 s58, s58A (inserted by TMA 2004 s52)
options_appraisal.evidence: Average cost of re-opening new road surface: £15,000-£50,000 per dig

options_appraisal.description: Install VMS boards on M65 approaches and key A-roads to warn of delays, suggest alternative routes, and show real-time journey times.
options_appraisal.status: No permanent VMS in Burnley area
options_appraisal.evidence: VMS reduces re-routing response time from 15min to 2min; 8-12% peak flow redistribution

options_appraisal.description: Issue £240 Fixed Penalty Notices for every permit breach (working without permit, overrunning, poor signing). Doubled from Jan 2026.
options_appraisal.legal_basis: Street Works (Charges and Penalties) Regulations 2025 (SI 2025/1074), in force 5 Jan 2026
options_appraisal.status: New powers available — enforcement rate unknown
options_appraisal.evidence: FPNs doubled to £240 (£160 early payment). Working without permit: £500

options_appraisal.description: Designate M65 approaches and town centre A-roads as Protected Streets under s61 NRSWA. Refuse new utility apparatus where alternatives exist.
options_appraisal.evidence: All motorways automatically protected. A-roads can be designated if strategic traffic need demonstrated
